1023	def _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4_sm120(
1024	    *,
1025	    query: torch.Tensor,
1026	    swa_kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
1027	    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1028	    sparse_indices: torch.Tensor,
1029	    compressed_kv_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1030	    swa_topk_lens: torch.Tensor,
1031	    extra_sparse_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1032	    extra_sparse_topk_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1033	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1034	    bmm1_scale: float,
1035	    bmm2_scale: float,
1036	    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1037	    kv_layout: Literal["HND", "NHD"],
1038	) -> torch.Tensor:
1039	    if bmm2_scale != 1.0:
1040	        raise ValueError("SM120 DSv4 sparse MLA does not support bmm2_scale")
1041	    if query.ndim in (3, 4):
1042	        num_heads, head_dim = query.shape[-2:]
1043	    else:
1044	        raise ValueError(f"Expected query.ndim == 3 or 4, got {query.ndim}")
1045	    if query.dtype != torch.bfloat16:
1046	        raise ValueError(
1047	            f"SM120 DSv4 sparse MLA only supports BF16 query, got {query.dtype}"
1048	        )
1049	    if head_dim != 512:
1050	        raise ValueError(f"Expected DSv4 query head dim 512, got {head_dim}")
1051	    if num_heads not in (8, 16, 32, 64, 128):
1052	        raise ValueError(
1053	            "Expected 8, 16, 32, 64, or 128 query heads for SM120 DSv4 "
1054	            f"sparse MLA, got {num_heads}"
1055	        )
1056	    if swa_topk_lens is None:
1057	        raise ValueError("backend='sparse' requires swa_topk_lens")
1058	
1059	    swa_kv_cache = _check_sm120_dsv4_kv_cache_layout(
1060	        swa_kv_cache, kv_layout, "swa_kv_cache"
1061	    )
1062	    if swa_kv_cache.dtype == torch.uint8:
1063	        if swa_kv_cache.size(-1) != 584:
1064	            raise ValueError(
1065	                "Expected packed SM120 DSV4 swa_kv_cache head dim 584, got "
1066	                f"{swa_kv_cache.size(-1)}"
1067	            )
1068	    elif swa_kv_cache.dtype != query.dtype:
1069	        raise ValueError(
1070	            f"swa_kv_cache dtype must match query dtype, got {swa_kv_cache.dtype} "
1071	            f"and {query.dtype}"
1072	        )
1073	    elif swa_kv_cache.size(-1) != 512:
1074	        raise ValueError(
1075	            f"Expected swa_kv_cache head dim 512, got {swa_kv_cache.size(-1)}"
1076	        )
1077	
1078	    if (extra_sparse_indices is None) != (extra_sparse_topk_lens is None):
1079	        raise ValueError(
1080	            "extra_sparse_indices and extra_sparse_topk_lens must be provided "
1081	            "together for backend='sparse'"
1082	        )
1083	    query_for_sm120 = query if query.ndim == 4 else query.unsqueeze(1)
1084	    out_for_sm120 = out if out is None or out.ndim == 4 else out.unsqueeze(1)
1085	
1086	    sparse_mla_segments: List[_SparseMLASegment] = [
1087	        _SparseMLASegment(indices=sparse_indices, lengths=swa_topk_lens)
1088	    ]
1089	    if extra_sparse_indices is not None:
1090	        if compressed_kv_cache is None:
1091	            raise ValueError(
1092	                "compressed_kv_cache is required when extra_sparse_indices is provided"
1093	            )
1094	        compressed_kv_cache = _check_sm120_dsv4_kv_cache_layout(
1095	            compressed_kv_cache, kv_layout, "compressed_kv_cache"
1096	        )
1097	        if compressed_kv_cache.dtype == torch.uint8:
1098	            if compressed_kv_cache.size(-1) != 584:
1099	                raise ValueError(
1100	                    "Expected packed SM120 DSV4 compressed_kv_cache head dim 584, "
1101	                    f"got {compressed_kv_cache.size(-1)}"
1102	                )
1103	        elif compressed_kv_cache.dtype != query.dtype:
1104	            raise ValueError(
1105	                "compressed_kv_cache dtype must match query dtype, got "
1106	                f"{compressed_kv_cache.dtype} and {query.dtype}"
1107	            )
1108	        elif compressed_kv_cache.size(-1) != 512:
1109	            raise ValueError(
1110	                "Expected compressed_kv_cache head dim 512, got "
1111	                f"{compressed_kv_cache.size(-1)}"
1112	            )
1113	        sparse_mla_segments.append(
1114	            _SparseMLASegment(
1115	                indices=extra_sparse_indices,
1116	                lengths=extra_sparse_topk_lens,
1117	                kv_cache=compressed_kv_cache,
1118	            )
1119	        )
1120	
1121	    result = cast(
1122	        torch.Tensor,
1123	        _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_sm120(
1124	            query=query_for_sm120,
1125	            kv_cache=swa_kv_cache,
1126	            workspace_buffer=workspace_buffer,
1127	            sparse_mla_segments=sparse_mla_segments,
1128	            out=out_for_sm120,
1129	            sm_scale=bmm1_scale,
1130	            sinks=sinks,
1131	            lse=None,
1132	            return_lse=False,
1133	            kv_scale_format="auto",
1134	        ),
1135	    )
1136	    if query.ndim == 3:
1137	        return result.squeeze(1)
1138	    return result
1139

1141	def trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4(
1142	    query: torch.Tensor,
1143	    swa_kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
1144	    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1145	    sparse_indices: torch.Tensor,
1146	    compressed_kv_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1147	    sparse_topk_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1148	    seq_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1149	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1150	    bmm1_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor] = 1.0,
1151	    bmm2_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor] = 1.0,
1152	    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1153	    kv_layout: Literal["HND", "NHD"] = "HND",
1154	    cum_seq_lens_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1155	    max_q_len: Optional[int] = None,
1156	    enable_pdl: bool | None = None,
1157	    swa_topk_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1158	    extra_sparse_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1159	    extra_sparse_topk_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1160	) -> torch.Tensor:
1161	    r"""Decode DeepSeek V4 sparse MLA.
1162	
1163	    The implementation is selected from the query device architecture.
1164	
1165	    On SM100/SM103, this calls the TRTLLM-GEN DeepSeek V4 sparse MLA kernels.
1166	    The query and both KV pools use head dim 512. The query may be BF16 or
1167	    per-tensor FP8 E4M3 and the output is BF16. The first 128 columns of
1168	    ``sparse_indices`` are SWA entries into ``swa_kv_cache``; remaining columns
1169	    are compressed entries into ``compressed_kv_cache``. ``sparse_topk_lens``
1170	    gives the total active sparse length for each query token and must include
1171	    the fixed 128 SWA entries. ``seq_lens`` provides the original KV sequence
1172	    length for the SWA validity window.
1173	
1174	    On SM120/SM121, this calls the packed sparse backend. ``swa_kv_cache`` is
1175	    the required packed uint8 SWA pool with 584 bytes per token. ``sparse_indices``
1176	    and ``swa_topk_lens`` describe the active SWA segment. To add a compressed
1177	    segment, pass ``compressed_kv_cache`` as another packed uint8 pool and pass
1178	    ``extra_sparse_indices`` with ``extra_sparse_topk_lens``. The SM120/SM121
1179	    path accepts BF16 query tensors and produces BF16 output.
1180	
1181	    Parameters
1182	    ----------
1183	    query : torch.Tensor
1184	        Dense query input ``[batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads, 512]``
1185	        or varlen query input ``[sum_q, num_heads, 512]`` when
1186	        ``cum_seq_lens_q`` is provided. SM100/SM103 accepts BF16 or FP8 E4M3;
1187	        SM120/SM121 accepts BF16.
1188	    swa_kv_cache : torch.Tensor
1189	        SWA KV cache. TRTLLM-GEN uses head dim 512; SM120 sparse uses packed
1190	        uint8 head dim 584. Layout follows ``kv_layout``.
1191	    workspace_buffer : torch.Tensor
1192	        Backend workspace buffer. Must be zero-initialized for first use.
1193	    sparse_indices : torch.Tensor
1194	        TRTLLM-GEN combined sparse table, or the SM120 sparse SWA segment.
1195	    compressed_kv_cache : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1196	        Primary/compressed KV cache in the same backend layout as
1197	        ``swa_kv_cache``. Required by ``trtllm-gen`` and by SM120 ``sparse``
1198	        when ``extra_sparse_indices`` is provided.
1199	    sparse_topk_lens : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1200	        Flattened total sparse MLA top-k lengths in query-token order, shape
1201	        ``[sum_q]``. Values must already include the fixed 128 SWA entries,
1202	        matching TRTLLM-GEN ``sparseMlaTopkLengths``, and must not exceed
1203	        ``sparse_indices.shape[-1]``. Required only by ``trtllm-gen``.
1204	    seq_lens : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1205	        Original KV sequence lengths, shape ``[batch_size]`` INT32. Required
1206	        only by ``trtllm-gen``.
1207	    bmm1_scale : Union[float, torch.Tensor]
1208	        Fused per-tensor scale for QK and softmax. Tensor form must be FP32.
1209	    bmm2_scale : Union[float, torch.Tensor]
1210	        Fused per-tensor scale for VO. Tensor form must be FP32.
1211	    sinks : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1212	        Optional attention sink logits, shape ``[num_heads]`` FP32.
1213	    kv_layout : Literal["HND", "NHD"]
1214	        Layout of both KV pools.
1215	    cum_seq_lens_q : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1216	        Cumulative query lengths for varlen query input, shape ``[batch_size + 1]``
1217	        INT32. When provided, dynamic top-k lengths are consumed in flattened
1218	        query-token order.
1219	    max_q_len : Optional[int]
1220	        Maximum query length in the varlen batch. Required with
1221	        ``cum_seq_lens_q``.
1222	    enable_pdl : Optional[bool]
1223	        Whether to enable Programmatic Dependent Launch. Used by the
1224	        TRTLLM-GEN path.
1225	    swa_topk_lens : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1226	        Active SWA segment lengths for SM120/SM121, shape ``[sum_q]`` INT32.
1227	    extra_sparse_indices : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1228	        Optional SM120/SM121 compressed segment indices into
1229	        ``compressed_kv_cache``.
1230	    extra_sparse_topk_lens : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1231	        Active compressed segment lengths for SM120/SM121, shape ``[sum_q]``
1232	        INT32.
1233	    """
1234	    backend = _resolve_dsv4_sparse_mla_backend(query.device)
1235	    if enable_pdl is None:
1236	        enable_pdl = device_support_pdl(query.device)
1237	    if isinstance(bmm1_scale, torch.Tensor):
1238	        if backend == "sparse":
1239	            raise ValueError(
1240	                "SM120/SM121 DSv4 sparse MLA expects bmm1_scale to be a float"
1241	            )
1242	        if bmm1_scale.dtype != torch.float32:
1243	            raise TypeError("bmm1_scale tensor must have dtype torch.float32")
1244	        bmm1_scale = bmm1_scale * log2e
1245	    if isinstance(bmm2_scale, torch.Tensor):
1246	        if backend == "sparse":
1247	            raise ValueError(
1248	                "SM120/SM121 DSv4 sparse MLA expects bmm2_scale to be a float"
1249	            )
1250	        if bmm2_scale.dtype != torch.float32:
1251	            raise TypeError("bmm2_scale tensor must have dtype torch.float32")
1252	
1253	    if backend == "sparse":
1254	        return _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4_sm120(
1255	            query=query,
1256	            swa_kv_cache=swa_kv_cache,
1257	            workspace_buffer=workspace_buffer,
1258	            sparse_indices=sparse_indices,
1259	            compressed_kv_cache=compressed_kv_cache,
1260	            swa_topk_lens=swa_topk_lens,
1261	            extra_sparse_indices=extra_sparse_indices,
1262	            extra_sparse_topk_lens=extra_sparse_topk_lens,
1263	            out=out,
1264	            bmm1_scale=float(bmm1_scale),
1265	            bmm2_scale=float(bmm2_scale),
1266	            sinks=sinks,
1267	            kv_layout=kv_layout,
1268	        )
1269	
1270	    if (
1271	        swa_topk_lens is not None
1272	        or extra_sparse_indices is not None
1273	        or extra_sparse_topk_lens is not None
1274	    ):
1275	        raise ValueError(
1276	            "swa_topk_lens, extra_sparse_indices, and extra_sparse_topk_lens "
1277	            "are only supported on SM120/SM121"
1278	        )
1279	    if sparse_topk_lens is None or compressed_kv_cache is None or seq_lens is None:
1280	        raise ValueError(
1281	            "backend='trtllm-gen' requires compressed_kv_cache, sparse_topk_lens, "
1282	            "and seq_lens"
1283	        )
1284	
1285	    (
1286	        swa_kv_cache,
1287	        compressed_kv_cache,
1288	        sparse_topk_lens,
1289	        batch_size,
1290	        q_len_per_request,
1291	        query_flat,
1292	        expected_out_shape,
1293	        cum_seq_lens_q,
1294	    ) = _check_dsv4_sparse_mla_inputs(
1295	        query,
1296	        swa_kv_cache,
1297	        sparse_indices,
1298	        compressed_kv_cache,
1299	        sparse_topk_lens,
1300	        out,
1301	        sinks,
1302	        kv_layout,
1303	        cum_seq_lens_q,
1304	        max_q_len,
1305	        allow_sm120_packed_kv=False,
1306	    )
1307	
1308	    if out is None:
1309	        out = torch.empty(expected_out_shape, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=query.device)
1310	
1311	    check_shape_dtype_device(
1312	        seq_lens, (batch_size,), torch.int32, query.device, "seq_lens"
1313	    )
1314	    if cum_seq_lens_q is None:
1315	        q_lens = seq_lens.new_full((batch_size,), q_len_per_request)
1316	    else:
1317	        q_lens = cum_seq_lens_q[1:] - cum_seq_lens_q[:-1]
1318	    if _validate_dsv4_sync_checks() and torch.any(seq_lens < q_lens).item():
1319	        raise ValueError(
1320	            "seq_lens must be greater than or equal to the per-request query "
1321	            "lengths so TRTLLM-GEN can derive the SWA-128 valid window"
1322	        )
1323	
1324	    primary_kv_cache = compressed_kv_cache
1325	    sparse_indices = sparse_indices.reshape(query_flat.size(0), -1).contiguous()
1326	    sparse_topk_lens = sparse_topk_lens.contiguous()
1327	
1328	    op = get_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()
1329	    run_func = getattr(op, "trtllm_paged_attention_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4", None)
1330	    if run_func is None:
1331	        raise RuntimeError(
1332	            "trtllm_paged_attention_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4 is not available. "
1333	            "Rebuild FlashInfer with the DeepSeek V4 sparse MLA TRTLLM-GEN launcher."
1334	        )
1335	
1336	    sm_count = get_device_sm_count(query.device)
1337	    run_func(
1338	        out,
1339	        query_flat,
1340	        primary_kv_cache,
1341	        swa_kv_cache,
1342	        workspace_buffer,
1343	        sparse_indices,
1344	        seq_lens.contiguous(),
1345	        sparse_topk_lens,
1346	        bmm1_scale,
1347	        bmm2_scale,
1348	        batch_size,
1349	        q_len_per_request,
1350	        sm_count,
1351	        enable_pdl,
1352	        workspace_buffer.numel() * workspace_buffer.element_size(),
1353	        sinks,
1354	        cum_seq_lens_q.contiguous() if cum_seq_lens_q is not None else None,
1355	    )
1356	    return out

1359	_trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4 = trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_dsv4

1364	    mod = gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()
1365	    op = mod.build_and_load()
1366	    setup_cubin_loader(mod.get_library_path())

1380	class BatchMLAPagedAttentionWrapper:
1381	    r"""Wrapper class for MLA (`Multi-head Latent Attention <https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.04434>`_)
1382	    PagedAttention on DeepSeek models. This kernel can be used in decode, and incremental prefill
1383	    and should be used together with `Matrix Absorption trick
1384	    <https://github.com/madsys-dev/deepseekv2-profile/blob/main/workspace/blog/optimizing-mla.md>`_:
1385	    where :math:`W_{UQ}` is absorbed with :math:`W_{UK}`, and :math:`W_{UV}` is
1386	    absorbed with :math:`W_{O}`.
1387	    For MLA attention without Matrix Absorption (``head_dim_qk=192`` and ``head_dim_vo=128``, which is
1388	    used in prefilling self-attention stage), please use
1389	    :class:`flashinfer.prefill.BatchPrefillWithRaggedKVCacheWrapper`.
1390	
1391	    More information about The Paged KV-Cache layout in MLA is explained in our tutorial
1392	    :ref:`MLA Page Layout <mla-page-layout>`.
1393	
1394	    For more details about the MLA computation, Matrix Absorption and FlashInfer's MLA implementation,
1395	    please refer to our `blog post <http://flashinfer.ai/2025/02/10/flashinfer-deepseek-mla.html>`_.
1396	
1397	    Example
1398	    -------
1399	    >>> import torch
1400	    >>> import flashinfer
1401	    >>> num_local_heads = 128
1402	    >>> batch_size = 114
1403	    >>> head_dim_ckv = 512
1404	    >>> head_dim_kpe = 64
1405	    >>> page_size = 1
1406	    >>> mla_wrapper = flashinfer.mla.BatchMLAPagedAttentionWrapper(
1407	    ...     torch.empty(128 * 1024 * 1024, dtype=torch.int8).to(0),
1408	    ...     backend="fa2"
1409	    ... )
1410	    >>> q_indptr = torch.arange(0, batch_size + 1).to(0).int() # for decode, each query length is 1
1411	    >>> kv_lens = torch.full((batch_size,), 999, dtype=torch.int32).to(0)
1412	    >>> kv_indptr = torch.arange(0, batch_size + 1).to(0).int() * 999
1413	    >>> kv_indices = torch.arange(0, batch_size * 999).to(0).int()
1414	    >>> q_nope = torch.randn(
1415	    ...     batch_size * 1, num_local_heads, head_dim_ckv, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda"
1416	    ... )
1417	    >>> q_pe = torch.zeros(
1418	    ...     batch_size * 1, num_local_heads, head_dim_kpe, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda"
1419	    ... )
1420	    >>> ckv = torch.randn(
1421	    ...     batch_size * 999, 1, head_dim_ckv, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda"
1422	    ... )
1423	    >>> kpe = torch.zeros(
1424	    ...     batch_size * 999, 1, head_dim_kpe, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda"
1425	    ... )
1426	    >>> sm_scale = 1.0 / ((128 + 64) ** 0.5)  # use head dimension before matrix absorption
1427	    >>> mla_wrapper.plan(
1428	    ...     q_indptr,
1429	    ...     kv_indptr,
1430	    ...     kv_indices,
1431	    ...     kv_lens,
1432	    ...     num_local_heads,
1433	    ...     head_dim_ckv,
1434	    ...     head_dim_kpe,
1435	    ...     page_size,
1436	    ...     False,  # causal
1437	    ...     sm_scale,
1438	    ...     q_nope.dtype,
1439	    ...     ckv.dtype,
1440	    ... )
1441	    >>> o = mla_wrapper.run(q_nope, q_pe, ckv, kpe, return_lse=False)
1442	    >>> o.shape
1443	    torch.Size([114, 128, 512])
1444	    """
1445	
1446	    _blackwell_auto_fallback_warned: bool = False
1447	
1448	    @classmethod
1449	    def _maybe_warn_blackwell_auto_fallback(
1450	        cls, device: torch.device, selected_backend: str
1451	    ) -> None:
1452	        if cls._blackwell_auto_fallback_warned:
1453	            return
1454	        major, minor = get_compute_capability(device)
1455	        if major < 10:
1456	            return
1457	        cls._blackwell_auto_fallback_warned = True
1458	        warnings.warn(
1459	            f"BatchMLAPagedAttentionWrapper: backend='auto' selected "
1460	            f"'{selected_backend}' on SM{major}{minor}, which is not Blackwell-native "
1461	            f"and gives poor MLA decode performance. "
1462	            f"For decode, use "
1463	            f"flashinfer.mla.trtllm_batch_decode_with_kv_cache_mla "
1464	            f"(Blackwell-native trtllm-gen); backend='cutlass' is the closest "
1465	            f"in-wrapper alternative but may be slower than this fallback for "
1466	            f"decode shapes.",
1467	            UserWarning,
1468	            stacklevel=3,
1469	        )
1470	
1471	    @flashinfer_api
1472	    def __init__(
1473	        self,
1474	        float_workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1475	        use_cuda_graph: bool = False,
1476	        qo_indptr: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1477	        kv_indptr: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1478	        kv_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1479	        kv_len_arr: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1480	        backend: str = "auto",
1481	    ) -> None:
1482	        r"""Constructor for BatchMLAPagedAttentionWrapper.
1483	
1484	        Parameters
1485	        ----------
1486	        float_workspace_buffer : torch.Tensor
1487	            The user reserved workspace buffer used to store intermediate attention results in
1488	            split-k algorithm. The recommended size is 128MB, the device of the workspace buffer
1489	            should be the same as the device of the input tensors.
1490	        use_cuda_graph : bool, optional
1491	            Whether to enable CUDA graph capture for the prefill kernels, if enabled, the
1492	            auxiliary data structures will be stored in provided buffers. The ``batch_size``
1493	            cannot change during the lifecycle of this wrapper when CUDAGraph is enabled.
1494	        qo_indptr : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1495	            User-reserved buffer to back the ``qo_indptr`` array, shape ``[batch_size + 1]``,
1496	            dtype ``int32``.  Only consulted when ``use_cuda_graph=True``.  The wrapper
1497	            copies into this buffer at :meth:`plan` time so capture-time pointers remain
1498	            stable.
1499	        kv_indptr : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1500	            User-reserved buffer to back the ``kv_indptr`` array, shape ``[batch_size + 1]``,
1501	            dtype ``int32``.  Only consulted when ``use_cuda_graph=True``.
1502	        kv_indices : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1503	            User-reserved buffer to back the ``kv_indices`` array, sized to the maximum
1504	            expected number of pages, dtype ``int32``.  Only consulted when
1505	            ``use_cuda_graph=True``.
1506	        kv_len_arr : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1507	            User-reserved buffer to back the ``kv_len_arr`` array, shape ``[batch_size]``,
1508	            dtype ``int32``.  Only consulted when ``use_cuda_graph=True``.
1509	        backend : str
1510	            One of ``"auto"``, ``"fa2"``, ``"fa3"``, ``"cutlass"``. Default ``"auto"``.
1511	
1512	            ``"auto"`` picks ``"fa3"`` on SM90a, else ``"fa2"``. On SM>=100 neither
1513	            is Blackwell-native; for MLA decode prefer
1514	            :func:`trtllm_batch_decode_with_kv_cache_mla`. The ``"cutlass"`` option
1515	            in this wrapper is the closest in-wrapper alternative but may be
1516	            slower than the fa2 fallback for decode shapes.
1517	
1518	            ``"cutlass"`` uses the SM100/SM110 CUTLASS MLA decode kernel. Only
1519	            ``float_workspace_buffer`` is required; ``run()`` takes a different
1520	            input layout (concatenated ``q_nope_pe`` / ``ckv_kpe_cache`` plus
1521	            ``kv_len`` and ``page_table``).
1522	        """
1523	        self._float_workspace_buffer = float_workspace_buffer
1524	        self.device = float_workspace_buffer.device
1525	
1526	        if backend == "cutlass":
1527	            self._backend = backend
1528	            return
1529	
1530	        self._int_workspace_buffer = torch.empty(
1531	            (8 * 1024 * 1024,), dtype=torch.uint8, device=self.device
1532	        )
1533	        self._pin_memory_int_workspace_buffer = torch.empty(
1534	            self._int_workspace_buffer.shape,
1535	            dtype=self._int_workspace_buffer.dtype,
1536	            pin_memory=True,
1537	            device="cpu",
1538	        )
1539	        self._use_cuda_graph = use_cuda_graph
1540	        self._qo_indptr_buf = qo_indptr
1541	        self._kv_indptr_buf = kv_indptr
1542	        self._kv_indices_buf = kv_indices
1543	        self._kv_len_arr_buf = kv_len_arr
1544	        if backend == "auto":
1545	            self._backend = determine_mla_backend(self.device)
1546	            self._maybe_warn_blackwell_auto_fallback(self.device, self._backend)
1547	        else:
1548	            self._backend = backend
1549	
1550	    @flashinfer_api
1551	    def plan(
1552	        self,
1553	        qo_indptr: torch.Tensor,
1554	        kv_indptr: torch.Tensor,
1555	        kv_indices: torch.Tensor,
1556	        kv_len_arr: torch.Tensor,
1557	        num_heads: int,
1558	        head_dim_ckv: int,
1559	        head_dim_kpe: int,
1560	        page_size: int,
1561	        causal: bool,
1562	        sm_scale: float,
1563	        q_data_type: torch.dtype,
1564	        kv_data_type: torch.dtype,
1565	        use_profiler: bool = False,
1566	    ) -> None:
1567	        r"""Plan the MLA attention computation.
1568	
1569	        Parameters
1570	        ----------
1571	        qo_indptr : torch.IntTensor
1572	            The indptr of the query/output tensor, shape: ``[batch_size + 1]``.
1573	            For decoding attention, the length of each query is 1, and the content
1574	            of the tensor should be ``[0, 1, 2, ..., batch_size]``.
1575	        kv_indptr : torch.IntTensor
1576	            The indptr of the paged kv-cache, shape: ``[batch_size + 1]``.
1577	        kv_indices : torch.IntTensor
1578	            The page indices of the paged kv-cache, shape: ``[kv_indptr[-1]]`` or larger.
1579	        kv_len_arr : torch.IntTensor
1580	            The query length of each request, shape: ``[batch_size]``.
1581	        num_heads : int
1582	            The number of heads in query/output tensor.
1583	        head_dim_ckv : int
1584	            The head dimension of compressed-kv.
1585	        head_dim_kpe : int
1586	            The head dimension for rope k-cache.
1587	        page_size : int
1588	            The page size of the paged kv-cache.
1589	        causal : bool
1590	            Whether to use causal attention.
1591	        sm_scale : float
1592	            The scale factor for softmax operation.
1593	        q_data_type : torch.dtype
1594	            The data type of the query tensor.
1595	        kv_data_type : torch.dtype
1596	            The data type of the kv-cache tensor.
1597	        use_profiler : bool, optional
1598	            Whether to enable intra-kernel profiler, default is False.
1599	        """
1600	        # Other 1-byte dtypes (uint8, fp4, e5m2) would JIT-map to non-FP8
1601	        # element types and silently take an unsupported code path inside
1602	        # the kernel, so allowlist exactly the dtypes the kernel can handle.
1603	        _SUPPORTED_MLA_KV_DTYPES = (torch.float16, torch.bfloat16, torch.float8_e4m3fn)
1604	        if kv_data_type not in _SUPPORTED_MLA_KV_DTYPES:
1605	            raise ValueError(
1606	                f"MLA kv_data_type {kv_data_type} is not supported. "
1607	                f"Supported dtypes: {list(_SUPPORTED_MLA_KV_DTYPES)}."
1608	            )
1609	        if kv_data_type == torch.float8_e4m3fn:
1610	            if self._backend != "fa3":
1611	                raise ValueError(
1612	                    "FP8 kv_data_type for MLA is only supported with the fa3 "
1613	                    f"backend on SM90, got backend={self._backend!r}."
1614	                )
1615	            # Backend selection is independent of the runtime device; FP8 MLA
1616	            # requires SM90 specifically.
1617	            major, minor = get_compute_capability(self.device)
1618	            if major != 9:
1619	                raise ValueError(
1620	                    "FP8 kv_data_type for MLA requires an SM90 (Hopper) device, "
1621	                    f"got SM{major}{minor}."
1622	                )
1623	            # Removing this guard exposes vec_cast<half, fp8_e4m3>, which
1624	            # exists but is untested for MLA — silent wrong output.
1625	            if q_data_type != torch.bfloat16:
1626	                raise ValueError(
1627	                    "FP8 kv_data_type for MLA currently only supports "
1628	                    f"q_data_type=torch.bfloat16, got {q_data_type}."
1629	                )
1630	            # Also enforced by static_assert in mla_hopper.cuh.
1631	            if head_dim_ckv != 512 or head_dim_kpe != 64:
1632	                raise ValueError(
1633	                    "FP8 kv_data_type for MLA currently only supports "
1634	                    "head_dim_ckv=512 and head_dim_kpe=64 (DeepSeek MLA), got "
1635	                    f"head_dim_ckv={head_dim_ckv}, head_dim_kpe={head_dim_kpe}."
1636	                )
1637	
1638	        self._cached_module = get_batch_mla_module(
1639	            self._backend,
1640	            q_data_type,
1641	            kv_data_type,
1642	            q_data_type,
1643	            qo_indptr.dtype,
1644	            head_dim_ckv,
1645	            head_dim_kpe,
1646	            use_profiler,
1647	        )

1649	        kv_indptr_host = kv_indptr.to("cpu")
1650	        kv_len_arr_host = kv_len_arr.to("cpu")

1660	            self._kv_indices_buf = kv_indices.to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
1661	            self._kv_len_arr_buf = kv_len_arr.to(self.device, non_blocking=True)

1676	            qo_indptr_host,

1724	    @flashinfer_api(trace=mla_paged_decode_trace)
1725	    def run(
1726	        self,
1727	        q_nope: torch.Tensor,
1728	        q_pe: torch.Tensor,
1729	        ckv_cache: torch.Tensor,
1730	        kpe_cache: torch.Tensor,
1731	        out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1732	        lse: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1733	        return_lse: bool = False,
1734	        profiler_buffer: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1735	        kv_len: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1736	        page_table: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
1737	        return_lse_base_on_e: bool = False,
1738	        o_scale: Optional[float] = None,
1739	        *,
1740	        ckv_scale: Optional[float] = None,
1741	        kpe_scale: Optional[float] = None,
1742	    ) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]:
1743	        r"""Run the MLA attention computation.
1744	
1745	        Parameters
1746	        ----------
1747	        q_nope : torch.Tensor
1748	            The query tensor without rope, shape: ``[batch_size, num_heads, head_dim_ckv]``.
1749	        q_pe : torch.Tensor
1750	            The rope part of the query tensor, shape: ``[batch_size, num_heads, head_dim_kpe]``.
1751	        ckv_cache : torch.Tensor
1752	            The compressed kv-cache tensor (without rope), shape: ``[num_pages, page_size, head_dim_ckv]``.
1753	            ``head_dim_ckv`` is 512 in DeepSeek v2/v3 models.
1754	        kpe_cache : torch.Tensor
1755	            The rope part of the kv-cache tensor, shape: ``[num_pages, page_size, head_dim_kpe]``.
1756	            ``head_dim_kpe`` is 64 in DeepSeek v2/v3 models.
1757	        out : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1758	            The output tensor, if not provided, will be allocated internally.
1759	            When ``o_scale`` is provided, this should be an FP8 tensor.
1760	        lse : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1761	            The log-sum-exp of attention logits, if not provided, will be allocated internally.
1762	        return_lse : bool, optional
1763	            Whether to return the log-sum-exp value, default is False.
1764	        profiler_buffer : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1765	            The buffer to store the profiler data.
1766	        kv_len : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1767	            The query length of each request, shape: ``[batch_size]``. Required when ``backend`` is ``cutlass``.
1768	        page_table : Optional[torch.Tensor]
1769	            The page table of the paged kv-cache, shape: ``[batch_size, num_pages]``. Required when ``backend`` is ``cutlass``.
1770	        return_lse_base_on_e : bool, optional
1771	            Controls the base of the returned LSE values when ``return_lse=True``.
1772	            If ``False`` (default), the LSE is returned in base-2
1773	            (``log2(sum(exp2(...)))``) to match the kernel's internal log-base.
1774	            If ``True``, the LSE is converted to natural-log base (``log(sum(exp(...)))``)
1775	            for compatibility with cascade-merging APIs that expect base-e LSEs.
1776	        o_scale : Optional[float]
1777	            FP8 output dequantization scale (``real = quantized * o_scale``).
1778	            When provided, ``out`` must be an FP8 tensor. Only supported with
1779	            the ``cutlass`` backend.
1780	        ckv_scale : Optional[float]
1781	            Per-tensor dequantization scale for the compressed-KV cache when
1782	            ``kv_data_type`` is FP8 (``real = quantized * ckv_scale``). Required
1783	            (together with ``kpe_scale``) for the FP8 KV cache path on the
1784	            ``fa3`` backend. Must be a finite positive value. Must not be
1785	            provided when ``kv_data_type`` is BF16/FP16.
1786	        kpe_scale : Optional[float]
1787	            Per-tensor dequantization scale for the rope-K cache when
1788	            ``kv_data_type`` is FP8 (``real = quantized * kpe_scale``). Same
1789	            usage rules as ``ckv_scale``.
1790	        """
1791	        if self._backend == "cutlass":
1792	            if return_lse:
1793	                raise ValueError("return_lse does not support cutlass backend for now.")
1794	            if profiler_buffer is not None:
1795	                raise ValueError(
1796	                    "profiler_buffer does not support cutlass backend for now."
1797	                )
1798	            if ckv_scale is not None or kpe_scale is not None:
1799	                raise ValueError(
1800	                    "ckv_scale / kpe_scale are only supported with the fa3 backend "
1801	                    "and FP8 kv_data_type."
1802	                )
1803	            self._cached_module = get_mla_module()
1804	            output_scale = 1.0
1805	            if o_scale is not None:
1806	                output_scale = float(o_scale)
1807	                if not math.isfinite(output_scale) or output_scale <= 0.0:
1808	                    raise ValueError(
1809	                        f"o_scale must be a finite positive value, got {o_scale}"
1810	                    )
1811	                if out is None:
1812	                    raise ValueError(
1813	                        "out tensor must be provided when o_scale is used for FP8 output."
1814	                    )
1815	                if out.dtype not in (
1816	                    torch.float8_e4m3fn,
1817	                    torch.float8_e5m2,
1818	                ):
1819	                    raise ValueError(
1820	                        f"out must be an FP8 tensor when o_scale is provided, got {out.dtype}"
1821	                    )
1822	                check_shape_dtype_device(out, q_nope.shape, None, q_nope.device, "out")
1823	            elif out is None:
1824	                out = torch.empty_like(q_nope)
1825	            else:
1826	                check_shape_dtype_device(
1827	                    out, q_nope.shape, q_nope.dtype, q_nope.device, "out"
1828	                )
1829	            q_nope_pe = torch.cat([q_nope, q_pe], dim=-1)
1830	            ckv_kpe_cache = torch.cat([ckv_cache, kpe_cache], dim=-1)
1831	            _check_cutlass_shape(q_nope_pe, ckv_kpe_cache, kv_len, page_table)
1832	            lse = torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.float32, device=self.device)
1833	            self._cached_module.cutlass_mla_paged_attention(
1834	                self._float_workspace_buffer,
1835	                out,
1836	                lse,
1837	                q_nope_pe,
1838	                ckv_kpe_cache,
1839	                kv_len,
1840	                page_table,
1841	                output_scale,
1842	            )
1843	            return out
1844	
1845	        if o_scale is not None:
1846	            raise ValueError(
1847	                "o_scale is only supported with the cutlass backend for now."
1848	            )
1849	
1850	        # The C++ launcher reinterprets tensor storage by the JIT-template
1851	        # type chosen at plan(); a dtype mismatch here silently produces
1852	        # wrong output.
1853	        if q_nope.dtype != self._q_data_type:
1854	            raise ValueError(
1855	                f"q_nope.dtype={q_nope.dtype} does not match the planned "
1856	                f"q_data_type={self._q_data_type}."
1857	            )
1858	        if q_pe.dtype != self._q_data_type:
1859	            raise ValueError(
1860	                f"q_pe.dtype={q_pe.dtype} does not match the planned "
1861	                f"q_data_type={self._q_data_type}."
1862	            )
1863	        if ckv_cache.dtype != self._kv_data_type:
1864	            raise ValueError(
1865	                f"ckv_cache.dtype={ckv_cache.dtype} does not match the planned "
1866	                f"kv_data_type={self._kv_data_type}."
1867	            )
1868	        if kpe_cache.dtype != self._kv_data_type:
1869	            raise ValueError(
1870	                f"kpe_cache.dtype={kpe_cache.dtype} does not match the planned "
1871	                f"kv_data_type={self._kv_data_type}."
1872	            )
1873	
1874	        # e4m3fn is the only FP8 dtype reachable here (plan() rejects others).
1875	        kv_is_fp8 = self._kv_data_type == torch.float8_e4m3fn
1876	        if kv_is_fp8:
1877	            if ckv_scale is None or kpe_scale is None:
1878	                raise ValueError(
1879	                    "ckv_scale and kpe_scale are required when kv_data_type is FP8."
1880	                )
1881	            ckv_scale_f = float(ckv_scale)
1882	            kpe_scale_f = float(kpe_scale)
1883	            if not math.isfinite(ckv_scale_f) or ckv_scale_f <= 0.0:
1884	                raise ValueError(
1885	                    f"ckv_scale must be a finite positive value, got {ckv_scale}"
1886	                )
1887	            if not math.isfinite(kpe_scale_f) or kpe_scale_f <= 0.0:
1888	                raise ValueError(
1889	                    f"kpe_scale must be a finite positive value, got {kpe_scale}"
1890	                )
1891	        else:
1892	            if ckv_scale is not None or kpe_scale is not None:
1893	                raise ValueError(
1894	                    "ckv_scale / kpe_scale are only valid when kv_data_type is FP8."
1895	                )
1896	            ckv_scale_f = 1.0
1897	            kpe_scale_f = 1.0
1898	
1899	        if profiler_buffer is None:
1900	            if self._use_profiler:
1901	                raise ValueError(
1902	                    "Profiler is enabled, profiler_buffer must be provided"
1903	                )
1904	        num_heads = q_nope.shape[1]
1905	        page_size = self._page_size
1906	        sm_scale = self._sm_scale
1907	        causal = self._causal
1908	        mask_mode = MaskMode.CAUSAL.value if causal else MaskMode.NON_CAUSAL.value
1909	        device = self.device
1910	        if out is None:
1911	            out = torch.empty_like(q_nope)
1912	        else:
1913	            check_shape_dtype_device(
1914	                out, q_nope.shape, q_nope.dtype, q_nope.device, "out"
1915	            )
1916	
1917	        if return_lse:
1918	            if lse is None:
1919	                lse = torch.empty(q_nope.shape[:2], dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
1920	            else:
1921	                check_shape_dtype_device(
1922	                    lse, q_nope.shape[:2], torch.float32, q_nope.device, "lse"
1923	                )
1924	        profiler_args = (profiler_buffer,) if self._use_profiler else ()
1925	        self._cached_module.run(
1926	            self._float_workspace_buffer,
1927	            self._int_workspace_buffer,
1928	            self._plan_info,
1929	            q_nope,
1930	            q_pe,
1931	            ckv_cache,
1932	            kpe_cache,
1933	            self._kv_indices_buf,
1934	            out,
1935	            lse,
1936	            mask_mode,
1937	            num_heads,
1938	            page_size,
1939	            sm_scale,
1940	            return_lse_base_on_e,
1941	            ckv_scale_f,
1942	            kpe_scale_f,
1943	            *profiler_args,
1944	        )
1945	
1946	        return (out, lse) if return_lse else out

1949	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1950	# Autotuning support for trtllm_batch_decode_with_kv_cache_mla
1951	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1952	
1953	# Trtllm-gen kernel has a hardcoded max_batch_size = 8192 cap in
1954	# csrc/trtllm_fmha_kernel_launcher.cu:200 (the counter-region semaphore array
1955	# is sized for this max). Profiling beyond this would alias semaphores and
1956	# produce non-representative measurements.
1957	_TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_MAX_BATCH = 8192
1958	
1959	# Size of the trtllm-gen workspace counter region (multi-block semaphores)
1960	# per csrc/trtllm_fmha_kernel_launcher.cu:200: max_batch_size * max_num_qo_heads
1961	# * sizeof(uint32_t) = 8192 * 256 * 4 = 8 MB. trtllm-gen places this counter
1962	# slab at the head of the workspace_buffer and self-resets it at the end of
1963	# every launch, so back-to-back trtllm-gen launches keep it valid without any
1964	# host-side zeroing.
1965	_TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_COUNTER_REGION_BYTES = 8192 * 256 * 4
1966	
1967	
1968	def _cute_dsl_workspace_view(workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
1969	    """Sub-view of the shared workspace that skips trtllm-gen's counter region.
1970	
1971	    cute-dsl carves its scratch from offset 0 of whatever buffer it is given;
1972	    offsetting past the 8 MB counter region keeps it from writing into the
1973	    bytes trtllm-gen needs zero on entry. Costs 8 MB of usable workspace
1974	    (callers should size the buffer accordingly; the recommended 128 MB has
1975	    ample headroom).
1976	    """
1977	    workspace_i8 = workspace_buffer.reshape(-1).view(torch.int8)
1978	    return workspace_i8[_TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_COUNTER_REGION_BYTES:]
1979	
1980	
1981	def _round_to_seq_len_bucket(x: int) -> int:
1982	    """Power-of-2 bucket for max_seq_len in autotune cache keys.
1983	
1984	    Collapses small variations across deployments so they can share cache
1985	    entries (e.g., max_seq_len=16384 and 16385 hash to the same bucket).
1986	    """
1987	    if x <= 1:
1988	        return 1
1989	    return 1 << (x - 1).bit_length()
1990	
1991	
1992	def _cute_dsl_max_supported_batch(
1993	    workspace_bytes: int,
1994	    q_len: int,
1995	    num_heads: int,
1996	    kv_lora_rank: int,
1997	    max_active_blocks: int,
1998	    candidate_max: int,
1999	) -> int:
2000	    """Largest batch the caller's workspace can support for cute-dsl MLA decode.
2001	
2002	    Cute-dsl's workspace requirement grows with B via the kernel's per-CTA
2003	    split-K state. Binary-search for the largest ``B <= candidate_max`` whose
2004	    ``get_workspace_size(...)`` fits in ``workspace_bytes``.
2005	    """
2006	    from ..cute_dsl.attention.wrappers.batch_mla import (
2007	        _get_split_kv_and_workspace_size,
2008	    )
2009	
2010	    lo, hi = 1, max(1, candidate_max)
2011	    while lo < hi:
2012	        mid = (lo + hi + 1) // 2
2013	        _, ws = _get_split_kv_and_workspace_size(
2014	            mid, q_len, num_heads, kv_lora_rank, max_active_blocks
2015	        )
2016	        if ws <= workspace_bytes:
2017	            lo = mid
2018	        else:
2019	            hi = mid - 1
2020	    return lo
2021	
2022	
2023	def _compute_mla_decode_buckets(
2024	    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
2025	    runner_names: List[str],
2026	    q_len: int,
2027	    num_heads: int,
2028	    kv_lora_rank: int,
2029	    device: torch.device,
2030	) -> Tuple[int, ...]:
2031	    """Compute the autotune bucket list from kernel/workspace limits only.
2032	
2033	    The cap is intentionally independent of the caller's ``query.shape[0]``
2034	    and ``kv_cache.shape[0]`` so that autotuning at any single batch size
2035	    still tunes the full range a runner can actually serve. Buckets above a
2036	    runner's individual cap are handled by that runner's ``get_valid_tactics``
2037	    returning ``[]`` for the affected profiles.
2038	
2039	    Caps:
2040	      - trtllm-gen kernel-hardcoded ceiling: 8192
2041	      - cute-dsl dynamic workspace cap (via binary search)
2042	    """
2043	    from ..fused_moe.utils import get_hybrid_num_tokens_buckets
2044	
2045	    cap = 0
2046	    if "trtllm-gen" in runner_names:
2047	        cap = max(cap, _TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_MAX_BATCH)
2048	    if "cute-dsl" in runner_names:
2049	        from ..cute_dsl.utils import get_num_sm
2050	
2051	        # cute-dsl gives up the counter region only when trtllm-gen shares the
2052	        # buffer, so its usable size excludes that reservation only then.
2053	        reserved = (
2054	            _TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_COUNTER_REGION_BYTES if "trtllm-gen" in runner_names else 0
2055	        )
2056	        cute_dsl_cap = _cute_dsl_max_supported_batch(
2057	            workspace_bytes=workspace_buffer.numel() * workspace_buffer.element_size()
2058	            - reserved,
2059	            q_len=q_len,
2060	            num_heads=num_heads,
2061	            kv_lora_rank=kv_lora_rank,
2062	            max_active_blocks=get_num_sm(device),
2063	            candidate_max=_TRTLLM_GEN_MLA_MAX_BATCH,
2064	        )
2065	        cap = max(cap, cute_dsl_cap)
2066	
2067	    return get_hybrid_num_tokens_buckets(max(1, cap))
2068	
2069	
2070	def _cute_dsl_incompatibility_reason(
2071	    query: torch.Tensor,
2072	    out_dtype: torch.dtype,
2073	    bmm1_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor],
2074	    bmm2_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor],
2075	    sinks: Optional[List[torch.Tensor]],
2076	    sparse_mla_top_k: int,
2077	    skip_softmax_threshold_scale_factor: Optional[float],
2078	    uses_shared_paged_kv_idx: bool,
2079	    qk_rope_head_dim: int,
2080	    kv_lora_rank: int,
2081	    page_size: int,
2082	    is_var_seq: bool,
2083	    return_lse: bool,
2084	    lse: Optional[torch.Tensor],
2085	    cute_dsl_impl: str = "auto",
2086	) -> Optional[str]:
2087	    """Return None if cute-dsl can handle this call, else a human-readable reason.
2088	
2089	    Used by both the explicit ``backend="cute-dsl"`` path (raises the reason
2090	    as a ``ValueError``) and the ``backend="auto"`` filter (silently drops
2091	    cute-dsl from the runners list).
2092	    """
2093	    cc = get_compute_capability(query.device)
2094	    if cc[0] < 10:
2095	        return f"cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) requires SM100+, got SM{cc[0]}{cc[1]}"
2096	    if isinstance(bmm1_scale, torch.Tensor):
2097	        return (
2098	            "cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) does not support tensor bmm1_scale, "
2099	            "please pass a float value"
2100	        )
2101	    if isinstance(bmm2_scale, torch.Tensor):
2102	        return (
2103	            "cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) does not support tensor bmm2_scale, "
2104	            "please pass a float value"
2105	        )
2106	    # Cute-dsl supports sinks via its modular variant (auto-promoted when
2107	    # sinks is set, per flashinfer.cute_dsl.attention.mla_dispatch). The
2108	    # public sinks contract is a single per-head tensor or a length-1 list;
2109	    # reject the ambiguous len>1 case here so the autotune dispatcher and
2110	    # the explicit backend="cute-dsl" path see the same error.
2111	    if isinstance(sinks, (list, tuple)) and len(sinks) != 1:
2112	        return (
2113	            f"cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) expects sinks to be a "
2114	            f"single tensor or a length-1 list/tuple; got len={len(sinks)}"
2115	        )
2116	    if sparse_mla_top_k > 0:
2117	        return "cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) does not support sparse_mla_top_k"
2118	    if skip_softmax_threshold_scale_factor is not None:
2119	        return (
2120	            "cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) does not support "
2121	            "skip_softmax_threshold_scale_factor"
2122	        )
2123	    if not uses_shared_paged_kv_idx:
2124	        return (
2125	            "cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel) does not support separate KV "
2126	            "page indices (uses_shared_paged_kv_idx=False)"
2127	        )
2128	    # LSE is supported on the monolithic path; the modular path raises a
2129	    # clear NotImplementedError in wrappers/batch_mla.py if it gets picked
2130	    # for an LSE request (e.g. when ``sinks`` forces the modular dispatch).
2131	    # We don't pre-reject here so that the common case
2132	    # (cute_dsl_impl="auto" + LSE + no sinks → monolithic) goes through.
2133	
2134	    _, q_len, num_heads, _ = query.shape
2135	    try:
2136	        from ..cute_dsl.attention.mla_dispatch import _resolve_impl
2137	
2138	        resolved_impl = _resolve_impl(requested=cute_dsl_impl, kwargs={"sinks": sinks})
2139	    except (ValueError, ImportError) as e:
2140	        return f"cute-dsl backend (MLA decode kernel): {e}"
2141	
2142	    try:
2143	        if resolved_impl == "monolithic":
2144	            from ..cute_dsl.attention.monolithic.mla_decode import _check_can_implement
2145	        else:
2146	            from ..cute_dsl.attention.wrappers.batch_mla import _check_can_implement
2147	
2148	        _check_can_implement(
2149	            torch_dtype=query.dtype,
2150	            torch_out_dtype=out_dtype,
2151	            page_size=page_size,
2152	            num_heads=num_heads,
2153	            seq_len_q=q_len,
2154	            kv_lora_rank=kv_lora_rank,
2155	            qk_rope_head_dim=qk_rope_head_dim,
2156	            is_persistent=not is_var_seq,
2157	            is_var_seq=is_var_seq,
2158	            is_var_split_kv=False,

from dataclasses import dataclass
import functools
import math
import os
import warnings
from typing import List, Literal, Optional, Sequence, Tuple, Union, cast, overload

import torch

from ..api_logging import flashinfer_api
from ..autotuner import AutoTuner, TunableRunner
from ..trace.templates.attention import (
    mla_paged_decode_trace,
    trtllm_batch_decode_mla_trace_dispatch,
    xqa_batch_decode_mla_trace,
)
from ..jit import gen_batch_mla_module, gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module, setup_cubin_loader
from ..jit.mla import gen_mla_module
from ..utils import (
    MaskMode,
    _check_block_tables_shape,
    check_shape_dtype_device,
    determine_mla_backend,
    device_support_pdl,
    get_compute_capability,
    get_device_sm_count,
    is_sm12x_supported,
    log2e,
)
from ..xqa import xqa_mla


def _check_cutlass_shape(q_nope_pe, ckv_kpe_cache, kv_len, page_table):
    if q_nope_pe.ndim != 3:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected q_nope_pe.ndim == 3, got {q_nope_pe.ndim}")
    if ckv_kpe_cache.ndim != 3:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected ckv_kpe_cache.ndim == 3, got {ckv_kpe_cache.ndim}")
    if kv_len.ndim != 1:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected kv_len.ndim == 1, got {kv_len.ndim}")
    if page_table.ndim != 2:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected page_table.ndim == 2, got {page_table.ndim}")
    B_q, H, D_q = q_nope_pe.shape
    D_ckv = ckv_kpe_cache.shape[2]
    if H != 128:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected 128 heads for q_nope_pe, got {H}")
    if D_q != D_ckv or D_q != 576:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Expected head dim 576 for q_nope_pe and ckv_kpe_cache, got {D_q} and {D_ckv}"
        )
    B_block_table, block_num = page_table.shape
    block_size = ckv_kpe_cache.shape[1]
    if B_q != B_block_table:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Expected batch size {B_q} for q_nope_pe and block_table, got {B_q} and {B_block_table}"
        )
    if block_num % (128 / block_size) != 0:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Expected block_num % (128 / block_size) == 0, got {block_num=} and {block_size=}"
        )


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MLAHeadDimensions:
    """
    The dimensions of a single MLA head.

    Args:
        qk_nope_head_dim (int): The number of input channels without positional information in non-absorb mode.
        qk_rope_head_dim (int): The number of channels carrying positional information for both absorb and non-absorb modes.
        v_head_dim (int): The number of value channels, which is also the output head dimension in non-absorb mode.
        kv_lora_rank (int): The dimension of the compressed key-value representation across heads.
    """

    qk_nope_head_dim: int
    qk_rope_head_dim: int
    v_head_dim: int
    kv_lora_rank: int


deepseek_mla_dimensions = MLAHeadDimensions(
    qk_nope_head_dim=128,
    qk_rope_head_dim=64,
    v_head_dim=128,
    kv_lora_rank=512,
)

smaller_mla_dimensions = MLAHeadDimensions(
    qk_nope_head_dim=64,
    qk_rope_head_dim=64,
    v_head_dim=128,
    kv_lora_rank=256,
)

supported_mla_head_dimensions = [deepseek_mla_dimensions, smaller_mla_dimensions]


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MLALayerDimensions:
    """
    The dimensions of an MLA layer.

    Args:
        head_dimensions (MLAHeadDimensions): The dimensions of a single MLA head.
        num_heads (int): The number of heads in the MLA layer.
    """

    head_dimensions: MLAHeadDimensions
    num_heads: int


supported_mla_layer_dimensions = [
    MLALayerDimensions(
        head_dimensions=deepseek_mla_dimensions, num_heads=128
    ),  # DSR1 dimensions
    MLALayerDimensions(
        head_dimensions=deepseek_mla_dimensions, num_heads=64
    ),  # GLM-5 dimensions
    MLALayerDimensions(
        head_dimensions=smaller_mla_dimensions, num_heads=32
    ),  # Smaller model dimensions
]


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class _SparseMLASegment:
    """Internal SM120 sparse MLA KV segment."""

    indices: torch.Tensor
    lengths: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    kv_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class _NormalizedSparseMLASegment:
    kv_cache: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    indices: torch.Tensor
    lengths: Optional[torch.Tensor]


def _normalize_optional_mla_sink(
    sinks: Optional[Union[List[torch.Tensor], Tuple[torch.Tensor, ...], torch.Tensor]],
    backend_name: str,
) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
    if sinks is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(sinks, (list, tuple)):
        if len(sinks) != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"{backend_name} expects sinks to be a single tensor or a "
                f"length-1 list/tuple; got len={len(sinks)}."
            )
        return sinks[0]
    return sinks


def _normalize_sparse_mla_indices_and_lens(
    *,
    indices: torch.Tensor,
    lens: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    batch_size: int,
    q_len_per_request: int,
    device: torch.device,
    name: str,
) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
    if indices.ndim == 3:
        sparse_topk = int(indices.shape[-1])
        expected_shape: Tuple[int, ...] = (
            batch_size,
            q_len_per_request,
            sparse_topk,
        )
        if tuple(indices.shape) != expected_shape:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected {name}.shape == {expected_shape}, got {tuple(indices.shape)}"
            )
        indices = indices.reshape(batch_size * q_len_per_request, -1)
    elif indices.ndim == 2:
        sparse_topk = int(indices.shape[-1])
        expected_shape = (batch_size * q_len_per_request, sparse_topk)
        if tuple(indices.shape) != expected_shape:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected flattened {name}.shape == {expected_shape}, got "
                f"{tuple(indices.shape)}"
            )
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must have ndim 2 or 3, got {indices.ndim}")
    if sparse_topk <= 0:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} requires top-k > 0")
    if indices.dtype != torch.int32:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must have dtype torch.int32, got {indices.dtype}")
    if indices.device != device:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be on device {device}, got {indices.device}")

    if lens is None:
        return indices, None
    if lens.dtype != torch.int32:
        raise ValueError(f"{name}_lens must have dtype torch.int32, got {lens.dtype}")
    if lens.device != device:
        raise ValueError(f"{name}_lens must be on device {device}, got {lens.device}")
    if lens.ndim == 2:
        expected_lens_shape: Tuple[int, ...] = (batch_size, q_len_per_request)
        if tuple(lens.shape) != expected_lens_shape:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected {name}_lens.shape == {expected_lens_shape}, got "
                f"{tuple(lens.shape)}"
            )
        lens = lens.reshape(-1)
    elif lens.ndim == 1:
        expected_lens_shape = (batch_size * q_len_per_request,)
        if tuple(lens.shape) != expected_lens_shape:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected flattened {name}_lens.shape == {expected_lens_shape}, "
                f"got {tuple(lens.shape)}"
            )
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"{name}_lens must have ndim 1 or 2, got {lens.ndim}")
    return indices, lens


def _normalize_sparse_mla_segments(
    *,
    sparse_mla_segments: Sequence[_SparseMLASegment],
    batch_size: int,
    q_len_per_request: int,
    device: torch.device,
) -> List[_NormalizedSparseMLASegment]:
    if not sparse_mla_segments:
        raise ValueError("backend='sparse' requires at least one sparse MLA segment")
    if len(sparse_mla_segments) > 2:
        raise NotImplementedError(
            "backend='sparse' currently supports at most two sparse MLA segments"
        )

    normalized: List[_NormalizedSparseMLASegment] = []
    for i, segment in enumerate(sparse_mla_segments):
        if not isinstance(segment, _SparseMLASegment):
            raise TypeError(
                "sparse_mla_segments must contain _SparseMLASegment instances; "
                f"got {type(segment)!r} at index {i}"
            )
        if i == 0 and segment.kv_cache is not None:
            raise ValueError(
                "sparse_mla_segments[0].kv_cache must be None; the first segment "
                "uses the function's kv_cache argument"
            )
        if i > 0:
            if segment.kv_cache is None:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"sparse_mla_segments[{i}].kv_cache is required for additional "
                    "sparse MLA segments"
                )
            if segment.kv_cache.device != device:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"sparse_mla_segments[{i}].kv_cache must be on device {device}, "
                    f"got {segment.kv_cache.device}"
                )

        indices, lengths = _normalize_sparse_mla_indices_and_lens(
            indices=segment.indices,
            lens=segment.lengths,
            batch_size=batch_size,
            q_len_per_request=q_len_per_request,
            device=device,
            name=f"sparse_mla_segments[{i}].indices",
        )
        normalized.append(
            _NormalizedSparseMLASegment(
                kv_cache=segment.kv_cache,
                indices=indices,
                lengths=lengths,
            )
        )
    return normalized


def _workspace_tensor_view(
    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
    *,
    byte_offset: int,
    shape: Tuple[int, ...],
    dtype: torch.dtype,
) -> Tuple[Optional[torch.Tensor], int]:
    if not workspace_buffer.is_contiguous():
        return None, byte_offset
    elem_size = torch.empty(
        (), dtype=dtype, device=workspace_buffer.device
    ).element_size()
    byte_offset = ((byte_offset + elem_size - 1) // elem_size) * elem_size
    numel = math.prod(shape)
    byte_end = byte_offset + numel * elem_size
    workspace_bytes = workspace_buffer.numel() * workspace_buffer.element_size()
    if byte_end > workspace_bytes:
        return None, byte_offset
    flat = workspace_buffer.view(torch.uint8)
    view = flat[byte_offset:byte_end].view(dtype).view(shape)
    return view, byte_end


def _sparse_mla_decode_workspace(
    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
    *,
    num_tokens: int,
    num_heads: int,
    d_v: int,
    topk: int,
    extra_topk: int,
) -> Tuple[Optional[torch.Tensor], Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
    if num_tokens > 64:
        return None, None
    split_tile = 64
    num_splits = (topk + split_tile - 1) // split_tile + (
        extra_topk + split_tile - 1
    ) // split_tile
    mid_out, offset = _workspace_tensor_view(
        workspace_buffer,
        byte_offset=0,
        shape=(num_tokens, num_heads, num_splits, d_v),
        dtype=torch.bfloat16,
    )
    if mid_out is None:
        return None, None
    mid_lse, _ = _workspace_tensor_view(
        workspace_buffer,
        byte_offset=offset,
        shape=(num_tokens, num_heads, num_splits),
        dtype=torch.float32,
    )
    if mid_lse is None:
        return None, None
    return mid_out, mid_lse


def _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_sm120(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
    sparse_mla_segments: Sequence[_SparseMLASegment],
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    sm_scale: float,
    sinks: Optional[Union[List[torch.Tensor], Tuple[torch.Tensor, ...], torch.Tensor]],
    lse: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    return_lse: bool,
    kv_scale_format: str,
) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]:
    if not is_sm12x_supported(query.device):
        raise ValueError(
            "SM120 sparse MLA requires SM120a (CUDA >= 12.8) or SM121a (CUDA >= 13.0)"
        )
    if query.ndim != 4:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected query.ndim == 4, got {query.ndim}")
    if kv_cache.device != query.device:
        raise ValueError(
            f"kv_cache must be on query device {query.device}, got {kv_cache.device}"
        )
    if workspace_buffer.device != query.device:
        raise ValueError(
            "workspace_buffer must be on query device "
            f"{query.device}, got {workspace_buffer.device}"
        )

    batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads, head_dim = query.shape
    if head_dim not in (512, 576):
        raise ValueError(
            "Sparse MLA supports DSv4 head_dim=512 or "
            f"DSv3.2/GLM head_dim=576, got {head_dim}"
        )
    if num_heads > 128:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected num_heads <= 128, got {num_heads}")

    segments = _normalize_sparse_mla_segments(
        sparse_mla_segments=sparse_mla_segments,
        batch_size=batch_size,
        q_len_per_request=q_len_per_request,
        device=query.device,
    )
    primary_segment = segments[0]
    extra_segment = segments[1] if len(segments) > 1 else None

    from ._sparse_mla_sm120 import _SparseMLAPagedAttentionRunner

    query_flat = query.reshape(batch_size * q_len_per_request, num_heads, head_dim)
    expected_out_shape = (batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads, 512)
    if out is None:
        out = torch.empty(expected_out_shape, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=query.device)
    else:
        check_shape_dtype_device(
            out, expected_out_shape, torch.bfloat16, query.device, "out"
        )
    out_flat = out.view(query_flat.shape[0], num_heads, 512)

    flat_lse_shape = (query_flat.shape[0], num_heads)
    nested_lse_shape = (batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads)
    user_lse = lse
    out_lse_arg = None
    if return_lse and lse is not None:
        if tuple(lse.shape) == flat_lse_shape:
            check_shape_dtype_device(
                lse, flat_lse_shape, torch.float32, query.device, "lse"
            )
        elif tuple(lse.shape) == nested_lse_shape:
            check_shape_dtype_device(
                lse, nested_lse_shape, torch.float32, query.device, "lse"
            )
            lse = lse.view(flat_lse_shape)
        else:
            raise ValueError(
                f"lse must have shape {flat_lse_shape} or {nested_lse_shape}; "
                f"got {tuple(lse.shape)}"
            )
        out_lse_arg = lse

    runner = _SparseMLAPagedAttentionRunner(
        max_num_tokens=query_flat.shape[0],
        max_num_heads=num_heads,
        kv_scale_format=kv_scale_format,
        device=query.device,
    )
    extra_topk = extra_segment.indices.shape[-1] if extra_segment is not None else 0
    mid_out, mid_lse = _sparse_mla_decode_workspace(
        workspace_buffer,
        num_tokens=query_flat.shape[0],
        num_heads=num_heads,
        d_v=512,
        topk=primary_segment.indices.shape[-1],
        extra_topk=extra_topk,
    )

    out_lse = runner.run(
        query_flat,
        kv_cache,
        primary_segment.indices,
        out_flat,
        float(sm_scale),
        topk_length=primary_segment.lengths,
        attn_sink=_normalize_optional_mla_sink(sinks, "backend='sparse'"),
        extra_kv_cache=extra_segment.kv_cache if extra_segment is not None else None,
        extra_indices=extra_segment.indices if extra_segment is not None else None,
        extra_topk_length=extra_segment.lengths if extra_segment is not None else None,
        out_lse=out_lse_arg,
        mid_out=mid_out,
        mid_lse=mid_lse,
        return_lse=return_lse,
    )

    if return_lse:
        return out, user_lse if user_lse is not None else out_lse
    return out


def _check_sm120_sparse_v32_kv_cache(kv_cache: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    if kv_cache.dtype != torch.uint8:
        raise ValueError(
            "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM backend expects packed uint8 kv_cache, "
            f"got {kv_cache.dtype}"
        )
    if kv_cache.ndim == 3:
        if kv_cache.size(-1) != 656:
            raise ValueError(
                "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects packed kv_cache last dim 656, "
                f"got {tuple(kv_cache.shape)}"
            )
        return kv_cache
    if kv_cache.ndim == 4:
        if kv_cache.size(1) != 1 or kv_cache.size(-1) != 656:
            raise ValueError(
                "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects public HND kv_cache shape "
                "[num_pages, 1, page_size, 656] or 3D shorthand "
                f"[num_pages, page_size, 656], got {tuple(kv_cache.shape)}"
            )
        return kv_cache
    raise ValueError(f"Expected kv_cache.ndim == 3 or 4, got {kv_cache.ndim}")


def _normalize_sm120_sparse_v32_topk_length(
    seq_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    *,
    batch_size: int,
    q_len_per_request: int,
    device: torch.device,
) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
    if seq_lens is None:
        return None
    if seq_lens.dtype != torch.int32:
        raise ValueError(f"seq_lens must have dtype torch.int32, got {seq_lens.dtype}")
    if seq_lens.device != device:
        raise ValueError(f"seq_lens must be on device {device}, got {seq_lens.device}")
    flat_tokens = batch_size * q_len_per_request
    if seq_lens.ndim == 2 and tuple(seq_lens.shape) == (
        batch_size,
        q_len_per_request,
    ):
        return seq_lens.reshape(flat_tokens).contiguous()
    if seq_lens.ndim == 1 and seq_lens.numel() == flat_tokens:
        return seq_lens.contiguous()
    if seq_lens.ndim == 1 and seq_lens.numel() == batch_size:
        return None
    raise ValueError(
        "seq_lens for SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM must be shaped either "
        f"({batch_size},), ({flat_tokens},), or "
        f"({batch_size}, {q_len_per_request}); got {tuple(seq_lens.shape)}"
    )


def _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_v32_sm120(
    *,
    query: torch.Tensor,
    kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
    qk_nope_head_dim: int,
    kv_lora_rank: int,
    qk_rope_head_dim: int,
    block_tables: torch.Tensor,
    seq_lens: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    sparse_mla_top_k: int,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    bmm1_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor],
    bmm2_scale: Union[float, torch.Tensor],
    sinks: Optional[List[torch.Tensor]],
    skip_softmax_threshold_scale_factor: Optional[float],
    uses_shared_paged_kv_idx: bool,
    lse: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    return_lse: bool,
    kv_scale_format: str,
) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]:
    del qk_nope_head_dim
    if sparse_mla_top_k <= 0:
        raise ValueError("SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM requires sparse_mla_top_k > 0")
    if skip_softmax_threshold_scale_factor is not None:
        raise ValueError("skip_softmax is not supported for sparse MLA")
    if not uses_shared_paged_kv_idx:
        raise ValueError(
            "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects shared sparse page indices "
            "(uses_shared_paged_kv_idx=True)"
        )
    if isinstance(bmm1_scale, torch.Tensor):
        raise ValueError("SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects bmm1_scale to be a float")
    if isinstance(bmm2_scale, torch.Tensor):
        raise ValueError("SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects bmm2_scale to be a float")
    if float(bmm2_scale) != 1.0:
        raise ValueError("SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM does not support bmm2_scale")
    if query.ndim != 4:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected query.ndim == 4, got {query.ndim}")
    if query.dtype != torch.bfloat16:
        raise ValueError(
            f"SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects BF16 query, got {query.dtype}"
        )
    if kv_lora_rank != 512 or qk_rope_head_dim != 64 or query.size(-1) != 576:
        raise ValueError(
            "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects kv_lora_rank=512, "
            f"qk_rope_head_dim=64, and query head dim 576; got "
            f"kv_lora_rank={kv_lora_rank}, "
            f"qk_rope_head_dim={qk_rope_head_dim}, query dim={query.size(-1)}"
        )
    if workspace_buffer.device != query.device:
        raise ValueError(
            "workspace_buffer must be on query device "
            f"{query.device}, got {workspace_buffer.device}"
        )

    batch_size, q_len_per_request, _, _ = query.shape
    if block_tables.dtype != torch.int32:
        raise ValueError(
            f"block_tables must have dtype torch.int32, got {block_tables.dtype}"
        )
    if block_tables.device != query.device:
        raise ValueError(
            f"block_tables must be on query device {query.device}, "
            f"got {block_tables.device}"
        )
    expected_block_tables_shape = (batch_size, q_len_per_request, sparse_mla_top_k)
    if tuple(block_tables.shape) != expected_block_tables_shape:
        raise ValueError(
            "SM120 sparse MLA v32/GLM expects sparse block_tables shape "
            f"{expected_block_tables_shape}, got {tuple(block_tables.shape)}"
        )

    kv_cache = _check_sm120_sparse_v32_kv_cache(kv_cache)
    topk_length = _normalize_sm120_sparse_v32_topk_length(
        seq_lens,
        batch_size=batch_size,
        q_len_per_request=q_len_per_request,
        device=query.device,
    )
    sparse_segment = _SparseMLASegment(
        indices=block_tables,
        lengths=topk_length,
    )
    return _trtllm_batch_decode_sparse_mla_sm120(
        query=query,
        kv_cache=kv_cache,
        workspace_buffer=workspace_buffer,
        sparse_mla_segments=[sparse_segment],
        out=out,
        sm_scale=float(bmm1_scale),
        sinks=sinks,
        lse=lse,
        return_lse=return_lse,
        kv_scale_format=kv_scale_format,
    )


def _check_trtllm_gen_mla_shape(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    kv_lora_rank: int,
    qk_rope_head_dim: int,
    sparse_mla_top_k: int,
    page_table: torch.Tensor,
    page_size: int,
    uses_shared_paged_kv_idx: bool = True,
    batch_size: Optional[int] = None,
    max_q_len: Optional[int] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    is_flattened_query = False
    if query.ndim == 4:
        num_seqs, num_tokens, _, qk_head_dim = query.shape
    elif query.ndim == 3:
        is_flattened_query = True
        if batch_size is None or max_q_len is None:
            raise ValueError(
                "batch_size and max_q_len are required when query.ndim == 3"
            )
        num_seqs = batch_size
        num_tokens = max_q_len
        _, _, qk_head_dim = query.shape
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected query.ndim == 3 or 4, got {query.ndim}")

    # Support both 3D and 4D kv_cache for backward compatibility
    if kv_cache.ndim == 3:
        # [num_pages, page_size, head_dim_ckv + head_dim_kpe] -> [num_pages, 1, page_size, head_dim_ckv + head_dim_kpe]
        kv_cache = kv_cache.unsqueeze(1)
    elif kv_cache.ndim != 4:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected kv_cache.ndim == 3 or 4, got {kv_cache.ndim}")

    is_deepseek_dimensions = (
        kv_lora_rank == deepseek_mla_dimensions.kv_lora_rank
        and qk_rope_head_dim == deepseek_mla_dimensions.qk_rope_head_dim
    )
    is_smaller_mla_dimensions = (
        kv_lora_rank == smaller_mla_dimensions.kv_lora_rank
        and qk_rope_head_dim == smaller_mla_dimensions.qk_rope_head_dim
    )
    if not (is_deepseek_dimensions or is_smaller_mla_dimensions):
        raise ValueError(
            f"Unsupported MLA dimensions, got kv_lora_rank={kv_lora_rank} and qk_rope_head_dim={qk_rope_head_dim}, supported dimensions are: {supported_mla_head_dimensions}"
        )

    ckv_dim = kv_cache.shape[3]
    expected_qk_head_dim = kv_lora_rank + qk_rope_head_dim
    if qk_head_dim != expected_qk_head_dim or ckv_dim != expected_qk_head_dim:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Expected head dim {expected_qk_head_dim} for query and kv_cache, got {qk_head_dim} and {ckv_dim}"
        )

    if sparse_mla_top_k > 0:
        page_table_shape = page_table.shape
        expected_page_table_shape = (
            (query.size(0), sparse_mla_top_k)
            if is_flattened_query
            else (num_seqs, num_tokens, sparse_mla_top_k)
        )
        if page_table_shape != expected_page_table_shape:
            raise ValueError(
                "Expected page_table.shape == "
                f"{expected_page_table_shape}" + f", got {page_table_shape}"
            )
    else:
        _check_block_tables_shape(page_table, uses_shared_paged_kv_idx)
        B_block_table = page_table.shape[0]
        block_num = page_table.shape[-1]
        block_size = page_size
        if num_seqs != B_block_table:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected batch size {num_seqs} for query and block_table, got {num_seqs} and {B_block_table}"
            )
        if block_num % (128 / block_size) != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected block_num % (128 / block_size) == 0, got {block_num=} and {block_size=}"
            )

    return kv_cache


def _normalize_dsv4_sparse_mla_kv_cache(
    kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    kv_layout: Literal["HND", "NHD"],
    name: str,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if kv_cache.ndim == 3:
        if kv_layout == "NHD":
            raise ValueError(
                f"{name} with ndim == 3 is only valid for kv_layout='HND'; "
                f"got kv_layout={kv_layout}"
            )
        kv_cache = kv_cache.unsqueeze(1)
    elif kv_cache.ndim != 4:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {name}.ndim == 3 or 4, got {kv_cache.ndim}")

    if kv_layout == "HND":
        # [num_pages, num_kv_heads, page_size, head_dim]
        if kv_cache.size(1) != 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected {name}.shape[1] == 1, got {kv_cache.size(1)}")
        return kv_cache
    if kv_layout == "NHD":
        # [num_pages, page_size, num_kv_heads, head_dim] -> HND strides.
        if kv_cache.size(2) != 1:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected {name}.shape[2] == 1, got {kv_cache.size(2)}")
        return kv_cache.transpose(-3, -2)

    raise ValueError(f"kv_layout must be either 'HND' or 'NHD', got {kv_layout}")


def _check_sm120_dsv4_kv_cache_layout(
    kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    kv_layout: Literal["HND", "NHD"],
    name: str,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if kv_cache.ndim == 3:
        return kv_cache
    if kv_cache.ndim != 4:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {name}.ndim == 3 or 4, got {kv_cache.ndim}")
    if kv_layout == "HND":
        if kv_cache.size(1) != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected packed SM120 DSV4 HND {name}.shape[1] == 1, got "
                f"{tuple(kv_cache.shape)}"
            )
    elif kv_layout == "NHD":
        if kv_cache.size(2) != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected packed SM120 DSV4 NHD {name}.shape[2] == 1, got "
                f"{tuple(kv_cache.shape)}"
            )
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"kv_layout must be either 'HND' or 'NHD', got {kv_layout}")
    return kv_cache


def _normalize_dsv4_topk_lens(
    topk_lens: torch.Tensor,
    batch_size: int,
    q_len_per_request: int,
    sum_seq_q: int,
    name: str,
    device: torch.device,
    cum_seq_lens_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if topk_lens.dtype != torch.int32:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must have dtype torch.int32, got {topk_lens.dtype}")
    if topk_lens.device != device:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be on device {device}, got {topk_lens.device}")
    if topk_lens.ndim != 1:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected flattened {name}.ndim == 1, got {topk_lens.ndim}")
    if topk_lens.size(0) != sum_seq_q:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Expected flattened {name}.shape == ({sum_seq_q},), "
            f"got {tuple(topk_lens.shape)}"
        )
    if cum_seq_lens_q is not None:
        check_shape_dtype_device(
            cum_seq_lens_q,
            (batch_size + 1,),
            torch.int32,
            device,
            "cum_seq_lens_q",
        )
    return topk_lens


def _validate_dsv4_sync_checks() -> bool:
    return os.environ.get("FLASHINFER_VALIDATE_INPUTS", "0") not in ("0", "")


def _check_dsv4_sparse_mla_inputs(
    query: torch.Tensor,
    swa_kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    sparse_indices: torch.Tensor,
    compressed_kv_cache: torch.Tensor,
    sparse_topk_lens: torch.Tensor,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    sinks: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    kv_layout: Literal["HND", "NHD"],
    cum_seq_lens_q: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    max_q_len: Optional[int],
    *,
    allow_sm120_packed_kv: bool = False,
) -> Tuple[
    torch.Tensor,
    torch.Tensor,
    torch.Tensor,
    int,
    int,
    torch.Tensor,
    Tuple[int, ...],
    Optional[torch.Tensor],
]:
    is_varlen_q = cum_seq_lens_q is not None
    out_shape: Tuple[int, ...]
    sparse_indices_prefix_shape: Tuple[int, ...]
    if is_varlen_q:
        if query.ndim != 3:
            raise ValueError(
                "Expected query.ndim == 3 when cum_seq_lens_q is provided, "
                f"got {query.ndim}"
            )
        if cum_seq_lens_q is None:
            raise ValueError("cum_seq_lens_q is required for varlen query input")
        if cum_seq_lens_q.dtype != torch.int32:
            raise ValueError(
                f"cum_seq_lens_q must have dtype torch.int32, got {cum_seq_lens_q.dtype}"
            )
        if cum_seq_lens_q.ndim != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected cum_seq_lens_q.ndim == 1, got {cum_seq_lens_q.ndim}"
            )
        batch_size = cum_seq_lens_q.numel() - 1
        if batch_size <= 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected cum_seq_lens_q.numel() >= 2, got {cum_seq_lens_q.numel()}"
            )
        if cum_seq_lens_q.device != query.device:
            raise ValueError(
                f"cum_seq_lens_q must be on query device {query.device}, "
                f"got {cum_seq_lens_q.device}"
            )
        sum_seq_q, num_heads, head_dim = query.shape
        if max_q_len is None:
            raise ValueError(
                "max_q_len is required when cum_seq_lens_q is provided to avoid "
                "an implicit device-to-host synchronization"
            )
        if max_q_len <= 0:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected max_q_len > 0, got {max_q_len}")
        q_len_per_request = max_q_len
        query_flat = query
        out_shape = (sum_seq_q, num_heads, 512)
        sparse_indices_prefix_shape = (sum_seq_q,)
    else:
        if query.ndim != 4:
            raise ValueError(f"Expected query.ndim == 4, got {query.ndim}")
        batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads, head_dim = query.shape
        sum_seq_q = batch_size * q_len_per_request
        if max_q_len is not None and max_q_len != q_len_per_request:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Expected max_q_len == {q_len_per_request} for dense query input, "
                f"got {max_q_len}"
            )
        max_q_len = q_len_per_request
        query_flat = query.flatten(0, 1)
        out_shape = (batch_size, q_len_per_request, num_heads, 512)
        sparse_indices_prefix_shape = (sum_seq_q,)
